title: Cross-modal Oddball Task.

funding_references.funder_name: and NRSA F32 AG069445-01 to RC. All research protocols were approved by the University of Iowa

description: A cross-modal oddball task dataset comprising 146 subjects (98 with Parkinson's disease, 48 controls) collected between 2017-2021. Participants performed a directional response task cued by visual arrows, preceded by synchronized visual and auditory pre-cues that varied between standard and oddball conditions. Analysis focused on trials with both pre-cues standard or oddball auditory pre-cue. Continuous 64-channel EEG was recorded throughout the experiment to investigate neural responses to cross-modal stimulus integration and oddball detection in the context of neurodegenerative disease.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a 64-channel BrainVision cap during a cross-modal oddball task. Each trial consisted of visual and auditory pre-cues presented simultaneously 500ms before a primary visual GO cue (white arrow) requiring a directional motor response. Pre-cue conditions included both standard, oddball visual, or oddball auditory variants. Analysis focused on trials with both pre-cues standard or with oddball auditory pre-cue. Data collection occurred from 2017-2021.
